series_desc: A three-volume series on the independent consulting life.
label: Vol. 1
title: Foundations
blurb: Thirty-two essays on the basics of surviving and thriving as an independent: getting oriented, bootstrapping a practice, and managing the consulting psyche.

series_desc: A three-volume series on the independent consulting life.
label: Vol. 2
title: Superstructures
blurb: Thirty-four essays on the wider landscape of free agency: the history and future of the gig economy, creative frames for client work, and the long haul.

series_desc: A three-volume series on the independent consulting life.
label: Vol. 3
title: The Yakverse Chronicles
blurb: Thirteen absurdist consulting tales — yaks, gigsters, and capricious clients in increasingly surreal engagements.

title: vgr: The Twitter Years (2007–22)
blurb: 102 chapters: a curated compendium of 396 singles plus 101 threads — the complete Twitter writings of Venkatesh Rao (@vgr).

title: Breaking Smart: How Software is Eating the World
blurb: 21 essays on the software revolution — how agile, networked approaches are disrupting industries and remaking society. In English, German, and French.

title: Mediocratopia
blurb: 13 essays on mediocrity as aspiration — optimization resistance, withheld reserves, and the courage to slouch toward a mediocracy utopia.

title: The Gervais Principle
blurb: The definitive Ribbonfarm series on organizational life through the lens of The Office — sociopaths, clueless middle management, and losers navigating institutional reality.

title: Be Slightly Evil
blurb: A Machiavellian guide to organizational life — essays on power, leverage, and strategic imperfection for those who live and work in institutions.

series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
label: Vol. 1
title: Tempo
blurb: The original 2011 book on decision-making under time pressure, reissued as the prequel to the Configurancy series.

series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
label: Vol. 2
title: Indexicality
blurb: The Clockless Clock — nine chapters on how we anchor ourselves in time without clocks, drawn from the Contraptions serialization.

series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
label: Vol. 3
title: Divergence
blurb: On temporal divergence — how timelines branch, histories fork, and alternative presents accumulate.

series_desc: A four-volume series on the nature of time: tempo, indexicality, divergence, and liveness.
label: Vol. 4
title: Liveness
blurb: On liveness — the phenomenology of the present tense and what it means to be in the live moment.

title: The Great Weirding
blurb: Essays on the chaotic transformation of the 2016–2020 era — the historical, cultural, and political weirdness that defines our present.

title: On Lore
blurb: Essays on lore, world-building, and the informal knowledge systems that hold communities together.

title: Mediocre Computing
blurb: Essays on computing as a creative and intellectual practice for the rest of us — below the frontier, above the mundane.

title: Breaking Smart Newsletter (2015–2019)
blurb: Newsletter archives from the Breaking Smart era — essays on software eating the world, collected from the active newsletter years.

title: Guerrilla Guide to Enterprise 2.0
blurb: From the defunct Enterprise 2.0 blog — a practitioner’s guide to social software in organizations.
